seo_title: Google Updates Spam Policy to Mark AI Manipulation Attempts
meta_description: Google has updated its spam policy to mark attempts to manipulate its AI model in search results as spam.
body_html:
Google has updated its spam policy to mark attempts to manipulate its AI model in search results as spam.

What is considered spam in Google's AI Search?

Attempting to rank content highly Manipulating generative AI responses Injecting biased 'best-of' listicles or 'recommendation poisoning'

Consequences for sites caught manipulating AI responses PenaltyDescriptionRanking lower in search resultsSites may see a decrease in their ranking position if they are found to be manipulating AI responses.Removal from search resultsSites that are caught manipulating AI responses may have their content removed from Google's search results altogether. Industry implications of Google's policy update Google's policy update has significant implications for the 'GEO' industry, which promises to get brands and websites regularly mentioned and cited by AI search tools. The updated policy makes it clear that strategies like biased 'best-of'

listicles or 'recommendation poisoning' are no longer acceptable.

source.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI
source.summary: Google updated its spam policy to mark attempts to "manipulate" its AI model in search results as spam, including results in AI Overview or AI Mode in Search, as Search Engine Land reports: "In the context of Google Search, spam refers to techniques used to deceive users or manipulate our Search systems into featuring content […]
